evals.prompt: I'm designing an E2E journey for my React app. Users can sign up, create a post, and see it in the feed. That's three actions. Should I make it one journey or three separate journeys?
evals.expected_output: Explain: one journey (one user goal: 'new user creates a post'). Precondition: no account. Actions: (1) sign up, (2) create post, (3) navigate to feed. Assertion: post visible in feed. It's atomic. If you split it into three journeys, you lose the end-to-end signal and can't catch the 'post created but not in feed' bug.

evals.prompt: My login journey passes most of the time but fails 1 out of 5 runs. Sometimes the dashboard takes forever to load. I've captured 5 runs of evidence. How do I figure out if it's a real bug or just my test being flaky?
evals.expected_output: Walk through the diagnosis: (1) Look at the PASS runs — does the dashboard load in <1s? (2) Look at the FAIL runs — does it take >3s? (3) Check the network tab in evidence — is the /api/dashboard endpoint actually slow on FAIL runs? If yes: real bug (API performance). If no: test issue (timing). Show how to compare screenshots and network timings, not just 'PASS' vs 'FAIL' verdicts.
